data.title: Semler Scientific Posts $65M Loss But Grows Bitcoin Holdings to 3,808 BTC
data.description:
On May 13, the healthcare company provided an update on its Bitcoin holdings and other key performance indicators (KPIs), reporting that it currently holds 3,808 BTC and has a Bitcoin Yield of 22% year to date.

The firm’s Bitcoin holdings are valued at $395 million at current market prices.

“We continue to accretively grow our Bitcoin arsenal using operating cash flow and proceeds from debt and equity financings,” said Eric Semler, chairman of Semler Scientific.

He added that the firm has launched a dashboard to provide the public with regularly updated information on its BTC holdings and other key metrics.

$SMLR reports first quarter 2025 financial results and acquires 174 #Bitcoins for $17.8 million and has generated BTC Yield of 22.2% YTD. Now holding 3,808 $BTC.

— Eric Semler (@SemlerEric) May 13, 2025

Semler Reports $65M Net Loss

Semleradded 894 bitcoins in Q1, worth around $91 million, and 616 more by May 12, worth almost $60 million. The firm began its BTC acquisition strategy in May 2024. Its most recent purchase was on May 12 when it bought 174 BTC. Its average acquisition price is $89,277 per BTC.

The firm is the twelfth-largest public company BTC holder, just behind Germany’s Bitcoin Group SE, according to BiTBO.

Semler CEO Doug Murphy-Chutorian said, “We are expecting growth and cash generation from FDA-cleared products and services, which will add to our Bitcoin treasury strategy.”

However, its revenue figures were not as pretty, with $8.8 million in revenue reported, representing a 44% decrease year-over-year. Semler reported a net loss of $64.7 million or $6.74 per share compared to a $6.1 million profit in Q1, 2024.

Company share prices (SMLR) were up marginally on the day but fell to $36 in after-hours trading. Semler stock has declined 32% since the beginning of 2025.

The firm also reported reaching a $30 million settlement agreement with the US Department of Justice regarding a civil investigative demand.Businesses Bet Big on Bitcoin

This week, Bitcoin investment firm River reported that businesses and corporations were acquiring BTC at a much faster pace than institutional funds and governments so far this year.

Stablecoin issuer Tetherpurchased 4,812 Bitcoinfor a total of $458.7 million for its new venture partnership, Twenty One Capital, this week.

Meanwhile, Michael Saylor’s Strategy scooped up an additional13,390 Bitcoinfor $1.34 billion on May 12.

Spot prices have inched up as a result, with the asset hitting an intraday high of $104,836 before retreating slightly during the Wednesday Asian trading session.

data.title: Akora Resources Names Peter Bird as Chief Executive
data.description:
Akora Resources appointed Peter Bird as chief executive, effective Friday, succeeding Paul Bibby, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Bird previously served as chairman of Zenith Minerals and CEO of Asiamet Resources.

data.title: Tolu Minerals Secures Papua New Guinea Tenement; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Tolu Minerals was granted the Ipi River tenement EL 2780 by the Mineral Resources Authority in Papua New Guinea, which is prospective for copper-gold mineralization, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

An airborne magnetotelluric survey was conducted over the eastern part of the license area to identify new geophysical targets related to gold and copper-lead-zinc mineralization, the filing said.

Shares rose 5% in midday trade Thursday.

data.title: Space42 Posts Higher Q1 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Space42 (ADX:SPACE42) said Wednesday that its profit and revenue for the first quarter surged year over year.

Profit attributable to the owners of the company for the three months ended March 31 was 80.2 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 10.3 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.0169 dirham from 0.004 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed space technology company's revenue was 422.6 million dirhams, compared with 113.3 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Malaysia PM discusses MH17 downing with Russia's Putin
data.description:
Malaysia Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said he discussed the downing of Malaysian Airlines Flight MH17 with Russian President Vladimir Putin during a visit to Moscow, days after a U.N. report found Russia responsible for the disaster.

A U.N. aviation council found this week that Russia was responsible for the downing of MH17, which was shot down over eastern Ukraine in 2014 during fighting between pro-Russian separatists and Ukrainian forces.

Moscow has rejected the International Civil Aviation Organization Council's (ICAO) findings on the crash that killed all 298 passengers and crew, calling the ruling biased.

Anwar said in a Facebook post late on Wednesday that he would continue to press for accountability over the disaster.

"Malaysia remains resolute in ensuring accountability and a just resolution for the victims and their families who continue to bear the weight of this tragedy," he said.

Anwar said that in the meeting Putin expressed condolences to the families of those killed, which included 196 Dutch citizens, 43 Malaysians and 38 Australian citizens or residents.

Putin called for a thorough and comprehensive investigation that is not politicised, Anwar said.

"I mentioned that this is a report made by ICAO, to which he (Putin) replied that from the beginning, he had requested that the investigation be independent and thorough."

Anwar said Putin told him that Russia is ready to provide its cooperation to ensure that the report is more credible or authoritative.

Dutch prosecutors accused Russia in a 2020 court case of trying to sabotage an investigation into the crash. The court later convicted two Russian intelligence agents and a Ukrainian separatist leader in absentia for their roles in the attack.

Anwar is in Russia for a three-day visit aimed at strengthening cooperation in areas such as trade and investment, agriculture, education, aerospace and energy.

data.title: Enel, Ansaldo Energia, Leonardo Launch Nuclear Power Research Company Nuclitalia
data.description:
Enel (ENEL.MI), Ansaldo Energia, and Leonardo (LDO.MI) signed the constitution of a nuclear power research and advanced technologies-focused company, Nuclitalia.

The new company will initially focus on water-cooled Small Modular Reactors, or SMRs, and will also explore industrial partnerships, according to a Wednesday release.

The Italian energy company owns a 51% stake in Nuclitalia, while power generation company Ansaldo Energia and defense group Leonardo own 39% and 10% interests, respectively.

data.title: Shinsegae's Net Attributable Income Plunges 44% in Q1; Shares Rise 3%
data.description:
Shinsegae posted first-quarter net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company of 57.5 billion won, down 44.07% from 102.9 billion won a year earlier, according to a Tuesday regulatory filing with the Korea Exchange.

The South Korean retail group's sales rose 3.81% year over year to 1.665 trillion won from 1.604 trillion won.

Shares of Shinsegae rose more than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Australia's Treasury Wine Estates hits near one-month low on CEO transition
data.description:
** Shares of Treasury Wine Estates fall 5.2% to A$8.65, their lowest level since April 22

** The Australian winemaker said Sam Fischer will take over as chief executive officer in October, succeeding Tim Ford

** Stock among top losers on benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index

** Around 5.1 mln shares change hands, vs the 30-day average of 4.3 mln shares

** Stock fell as much as 7.9% earlier in the day, marking its weakest trading session since mid-February

** CEO transition increases risk to longer-term targets given persistent U.S. market weakness and building China inventory - Jefferies

** Stock down 24.1% this year, including the session's move

data.title: Petra Diamonds Completes Exit from Williamson Diamonds
data.description:
Petra Diamonds completed the $16 million sale of all its shareholding in Williamson Diamonds to Pink Diamonds Investments, according to a Wednesday filing.

Completion follows the Tanzanian Fair Competition Commission's approval of the transaction. Petra intends to use the net proceeds from the sale for general corporate expenditures.

data.title: Battery recycler Li-Cycle files for bankruptcy protection in Canada
data.description:
Toronto-based lithium battery recycling firm Li-Cycle said on Wednesday that it has filed for bankruptcy protection in Canada and expects to start a formal sale for its business or assets.

The firm's U.S. units have also commenced proceedings in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York.

Li-Cycle has entered into a $10.5 million debtor-in-possession financing and a stalking horse credit bid for at least $40 million with London-listed Glencore , its largest secured creditor.

In March, Li-Cycle said it is seeking buyers for its business or assets as it needs additional funding to maintain operations. Glencore made an offer to acquire the Toronto-based company as part of a proposal to offset operational and financial issues.

The company has filed for bankruptcy protection under Chapter 15 and the Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act (CCCAA), a Canadian federal law that allows insolvent corporations that owe creditors in excess of $5 million to restructure their business and financial affairs.

In November last year, the U.S. Department of Energy finalized a $475 million loan for Li-Cycle, a financial lifeline for the company that kept running into cost overruns and technical issues.

data.title: Chicago soybeans retreat from nearly 10-month high as soyoil slumps; wheat rises
data.description:
Chicago soybean futures fell on Thursday from the nearly 10-month high hit in the last session, weighed down by a sharp decline in soyoil prices following crude oil's drop on a surprise U.S. inventory build and renewed hopes for a U.S.-Iran nuclear deal.

Soybean oil fell 4.8% to 49.81 cents per pound, marking the steepest daily percentage drop since July 2024.

The most-active CBOT soybean contract eased 0.97% to $10.67 a bushel, ending a five-session winning streak.

Oil prices lost nearly $1 in early trade, indirectly weighing on biofuel-related commodities like soybean oil and corn.

Optimism over a temporary truce in the U.S.-China trade war lent some support to soybean prices, but traders and analysts warned of uncertainty ahead of the U.S. marketing season.

On Wednesday, agribusiness consultancy AgResource warned U.S. soybean exports may drop 20% and that prices will plunge if the U.S. and China fail to resolve their trade dispute.

Corn prices are under pressure from the imminent Brazilian harvest, though concerns about potential unfavourable weather during the U.S. summer have provided some underlying support.

Brazil's 2024-25 corn output is estimated to be about 125 million metric tons, according to the Brazilian Association of Corn and Sorghum Producers.

Wheat rose 0.05% to $5.25 a bushel, stabilising after hitting its lowest in nearly five years on Wednesday.

"Wheat is just stabilising after a big run-up overnight, said Ole Houe, director of advisory services at IKON Commodities in Sydney, adding that prices will likely strengthen further due to dryness concerns in hard red winter wheat growing areas.

Traders are waiting for the U.S. Department of Agriculture's weekly export sales report, due later in the day.

Commodity funds were net buyers of corn, wheat, soybean and soyoil futures contracts on Wednesday, traders said. They were net sellers of soymeal futures, traders said. (COMFUND/CBT)

data.title: Singapore's CCIC Among List of US Sanctioned Companies
data.description:
CCIC Singapore was among more than 20 companies sanctioned by the US for its role in transporting Iranian oil, according to an updated list published by the US Department of the Treasury on Tuesday.

The company allegedly transported Iranian oil to China through Sepehr Energy Jahan Nama Pars Company and disguised it as a Malaysian oil shipment.

data.title: Australia's Syrah Resources rises as flagship graphite operation to restart
data.description:
** Shares of Syrah Resources rise as much as 3.9% to A$0.4, their highest level since June 17, 2024

** Industrial minerals and technology firm says its flagship Balama graphite operation in Mozambique is targeted to restart production by end of June quarter

** A force majeure event was declared in December at the graphite operations due to disruptions from protests exacerbated by ongoing civil unrest

** Stock extends YTD gains to 92.7%

data.title: Sanofi to Invest $20 Billion in US Drug Research, Manufacturing Through 2030
data.description:
Sanofi plans to invest $20 billion in drug research and development and manufacturing in the US through 2030, according to a Wednesday release.

The French pharmaceutical company's total investment includes a substantial increase in US-based R&D to speed up scientific advancements, alongside an expansion of domestic manufacturing capacity through direct investments in its facilities and partnerships.

data.title: Australia 10-Year Bond Yield Hits 4-Month High
data.description:
Australia’s 10-year government bond yield rose to around 4.59% on Thursday, marking its highest level in four months, after a surprisingly strong labor reading.

The Australian economy added 89,000 jobs in April, significantly surpassing expectations of 20,000 and March’s upwardly revised gains of 36,400.

Meanwhile, the unemployment rate held steady at 4.1%, in line with forecasts.

Data on Wednesday also showed a stronger-than-expected wage growth.

These reports signaled that Australia’s labor market remained tight, providing the Reserve Bank of Australia with lesser impetus to lower interest rates.

The recent easing of trade tensions between the US and China, which alleviated concerns about a global recession, also reduced the need for aggressive monetary easing at home.

Still, markets expect the central bank will cut its 4.10% cash rate by 25bps next week.

data.title: Aspen Group Seeks Up to AU$74 Million via Placement, Share Purchase Plan; Maintains Fiscal Year 2025 Underlying Earnings Guidance
data.description:
Aspen Group is raising up to AU$74 million through a placement and a share purchase plan to improve its growth prospects and meet increasing demand for affordable housing in Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The equity raising consists of an institutional placement to raise AU$70 million and a securityholder purchase plan to raise up to AU$4 million, the filing stated. Both are priced at AU$2.90 per APZ security and are non-underwritten, it added.

MST Financial Services and Aitken Mount Capital Partners are the placement's joint lead managers.

In the same filing, Aspen maintained its full fiscal year underlying operating earnings guidance of AU$0.167 per share, a 21% increase year on year. It also reaffirmed its dividend per security guidance of AU$0.10, up 18% on FY2024.

Net rental income for the first 10 months of the fiscal year ended April 30 was AU$29.3 million, up 13% on the prior year.

data.title: Market Chatter: Mapletree Investments Acquires Logistics Site in Chicago, US
data.description:
Mapletree Investments, a subsidiary of Singaporean state-owned multinational investment firm Temasek, acquired an 18.11-acre site located in Chicago, US, according to a report by the Singapore Business Review on Wednesday.

The company plans to develop a LEED certified logistics facility on the site, covering an area of 276,000 square foot, the report added.

Following the acquisition, the company now has 65 assets in Chicago with a total covered area of around 10.3 million square feet, the report noted.

data.title: China Vanke Secures Up to 1.6-Billion-Yuan Loan from Shenzhen Metro Group
data.description:
China Vanke secured a loan of up to 1.55 billion yuan from its largest shareholder, Shenzhen Metro Group, to repay its bonds' principal and interest.

The 36-month loan has a floating rate based on the one-year loan prime rate minus 76 basis points, currently set at 2.34%, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shenzhen Metro Group holds a 27.2% stake in the residential development and property services company.
